---

title: "GUIDE04: Environment Usage Patterns in R"
subtitle: "Creating and managing separate environments for modular R applications"
chapter: "CH17"
category: "guide"
number: "GUIDE04"
date-created: "2025-08-23"
date-modified: "2025-08-23"
author: "Claude"
type: "guide"
law: "Implementation Patterns"
article-number: "Guide 4"
related_to:
  - "MP016_modularity"
  - "MP017_separation_of_concerns"
  - "DEV_R002_functional_encapsulation"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Overview {#overview}

This guide documents the principle "Create a new environment if we want R to do something separately" with consistent naming conventions and object-oriented properties.

## Core Principle {#principle}

Environments in R provide isolated namespaces for:
- Configuration management
- Cache storage
- State management
- Module isolation
- Avoiding global namespace pollution

## Naming Conventions {#naming}

### Standard Environment Naming

```r
# Environment names should:
# - Begin with a period (.) to indicate semi-private status
# - Use CamelCase for the descriptive part
# - End with "Env" suffix to clearly identify as environment

.ConfigEnv <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())
.CacheEnv <- new.env(parent = globalenv())
.LoggingEnv <- new.env(parent = globalenv())
.StateEnv <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())
.ModuleEnv <- new.env(parent = globalenv())
```

### Common Environment Names

| Pattern | Purpose | Parent |
|---------|---------|--------|
| `.InitEnv` | Initialization parameters | `emptyenv()` |
| `.ConfigEnv` | Configuration storage | `emptyenv()` |
| `.CacheEnv` | Cached computations | `globalenv()` |
| `.TempEnv` | Temporary storage | `emptyenv()` |
| `.StateEnv` | Application state | `emptyenv()` |
| `.ModuleEnv` | Module namespace | `globalenv()` |

## Parent Environment Inheritance {#inheritance}

### Inheritance Patterns

```r
# 1. Isolated environment (no inheritance)
.IsolatedEnv <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())
.IsolatedEnv$x <- 10
# Cannot access variables from other environments

# 2. Global inheritance
.SharedEnv <- new.env(parent = globalenv())
global_var <- "visible to SharedEnv"
.SharedEnv$local_var <- "only in SharedEnv"
# .SharedEnv can access global_var

# 3. Custom parent chain
.ParentEnv <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())
.ParentEnv$parent_var <- "parent value"

.ChildEnv <- new.env(parent = .ParentEnv)
# .ChildEnv can access parent_var

# 4. Hierarchical structure
.AppEnv <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())
.ModuleEnv <- new.env(parent = .AppEnv)
.ComponentEnv <- new.env(parent = .ModuleEnv)
# Creates searchpath: .ComponentEnv -> .ModuleEnv -> .AppEnv -> emptyenv()
```

### Inheritance Decision Tree

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Create Environment] --> B{Need parent variables?}
    B -->|No| C[parent = emptyenv()]
    B -->|Yes| D{Which variables?}
    D -->|Global only| E[parent = globalenv()]
    D -->|Specific env| F[parent = .SpecificEnv]
    D -->|Package namespace| G[parent = asNamespace('pkg')]
```

## Object-Oriented Properties {#oop}

### Environment as Object

```r
# Create a counter object using environment
create_counter <- function(initial = 0) {
  .CounterEnv <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())
  .CounterEnv$count <- initial
  
  .CounterEnv$increment <- function() {
    .CounterEnv$count <- .CounterEnv$count + 1
    .CounterEnv$count
  }
  
  .CounterEnv$decrement <- function() {
    .CounterEnv$count <- .CounterEnv$count - 1
    .CounterEnv$count
  }
  
  .CounterEnv$get <- function() {
    .CounterEnv$count
  }
  
  .CounterEnv$reset <- function() {
    .CounterEnv$count <- initial
  }
  
  .CounterEnv
}

# Usage
counter <- create_counter(10)
counter$increment()  # 11
counter$get()        # 11
counter$reset()      # Back to 10
```

### Method Binding

```r
# Bind methods to environment
.DataStoreEnv <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())
.DataStoreEnv$data <- list()

# Add methods
.DataStoreEnv$add <- function(key, value) {
  .DataStoreEnv$data[[key]] <- value
}

.DataStoreEnv$get <- function(key) {
  .DataStoreEnv$data[[key]]
}

.DataStoreEnv$remove <- function(key) {
  .DataStoreEnv$data[[key]] <- NULL
}

.DataStoreEnv$list_keys <- function() {
  names(.DataStoreEnv$data)
}
```

## Practical Patterns {#patterns}

### 1. Configuration Management

```r
# Global configuration environment
.ConfigEnv <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())

# Load configuration
load_config <- function(file = "config.yaml") {
  config <- yaml::read_yaml(file)
  list2env(config, envir = .ConfigEnv)
  invisible(.ConfigEnv)
}

# Access configuration
get_config <- function(key, default = NULL) {
  if (exists(key, envir = .ConfigEnv)) {
    get(key, envir = .ConfigEnv)
  } else {
    default
  }
}

# Update configuration
set_config <- function(key, value) {
  assign(key, value, envir = .ConfigEnv)
}
```

### 2. Cache Management

```r
# Cache environment with TTL
.CacheEnv <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())
.CacheEnv$.timestamps <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())

cache_set <- function(key, value, ttl = 3600) {
  assign(key, value, envir = .CacheEnv)
  assign(key, Sys.time(), envir = .CacheEnv$.timestamps)
}

cache_get <- function(key, ttl = 3600) {
  if (!exists(key, envir = .CacheEnv)) return(NULL)
  
  # Check TTL
  timestamp <- get(key, envir = .CacheEnv$.timestamps)
  if (Sys.time() - timestamp > ttl) {
    rm(list = key, envir = .CacheEnv)
    rm(list = key, envir = .CacheEnv$.timestamps)
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  get(key, envir = .CacheEnv)
}

cache_clear <- function() {
  rm(list = ls(envir = .CacheEnv), envir = .CacheEnv)
  rm(list = ls(envir = .CacheEnv$.timestamps), 
     envir = .CacheEnv$.timestamps)
}
```

### 3. Module Isolation

```r
# Create isolated module environment
create_module <- function(name) {
  .ModuleEnv <- new.env(parent = globalenv())
  .ModuleEnv$.name <- name
  .ModuleEnv$.private <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())
  
  # Public interface
  .ModuleEnv$init <- function(...) {
    # Initialize module
    .ModuleEnv$.private$initialized <- TRUE
    list2env(list(...), envir = .ModuleEnv$.private)
  }
  
  .ModuleEnv$execute <- function() {
    if (!isTRUE(.ModuleEnv$.private$initialized)) {
      stop("Module not initialized")
    }
    # Module logic here
  }
  
  .ModuleEnv
}

# Usage
data_module <- create_module("DataProcessor")
data_module$init(source = "database")
data_module$execute()
```

### 4. State Management

```r
# Application state environment
.StateEnv <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())
.StateEnv$.observers <- list()

# State management functions
update_state <- function(key, value) {
  old_value <- if (exists(key, envir = .StateEnv)) {
    get(key, envir = .StateEnv)
  } else {
    NULL
  }
  
  assign(key, value, envir = .StateEnv)
  
  # Notify observers
  for (observer in .StateEnv$.observers) {
    if (!is.null(observer[[key]])) {
      observer[[key]](old_value, value)
    }
  }
}

get_state <- function(key, default = NULL) {
  if (exists(key, envir = .StateEnv)) {
    get(key, envir = .StateEnv)
  } else {
    default
  }
}

observe_state <- function(key, callback) {
  id <- length(.StateEnv$.observers) + 1
  .StateEnv$.observers[[id]] <- list()
  .StateEnv$.observers[[id]][[key]] <- callback
  id  # Return observer ID
}
```

### 5. Resource Management

```r
# Resource pool environment
.ResourcePoolEnv <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())
.ResourcePoolEnv$connections <- list()
.ResourcePoolEnv$max_connections <- 10

acquire_connection <- function() {
  if (length(.ResourcePoolEnv$connections) >= 
      .ResourcePoolEnv$max_connections) {
    stop("Connection pool exhausted")
  }
  
  conn <- DBI::dbConnect(...)  # Create connection
  id <- length(.ResourcePoolEnv$connections) + 1
  .ResourcePoolEnv$connections[[id]] <- conn
  
  id  # Return connection ID
}

release_connection <- function(id) {
  if (id <= length(.ResourcePoolEnv$connections)) {
    DBI::dbDisconnect(.ResourcePoolEnv$connections[[id]])
    .ResourcePoolEnv$connections[[id]] <- NULL
  }
}

# Cleanup on exit
on.exit({
  for (conn in .ResourcePoolEnv$connections) {
    if (!is.null(conn)) {
      try(DBI::dbDisconnect(conn), silent = TRUE)
    }
  }
})
```

## Environment Utilities {#utilities}

### Inspection Functions

```r
# List environment contents
env_ls <- function(env) {
  ls(envir = env, all.names = TRUE)
}

# Get environment size
env_size <- function(env) {
  sum(sapply(ls(envir = env), function(x) {
    object.size(get(x, envir = env))
  }))
}

# Copy environment
env_copy <- function(env, parent = parent.env(env)) {
  new_env <- new.env(parent = parent)
  for (name in ls(envir = env, all.names = TRUE)) {
    assign(name, get(name, envir = env), envir = new_env)
  }
  new_env
}

# Clear environment
env_clear <- function(env) {
  rm(list = ls(envir = env, all.names = TRUE), envir = env)
}
```

### Debugging Helpers

```r
# Print environment hierarchy
env_hierarchy <- function(env) {
  envs <- list()
  current <- env
  while (!identical(current, emptyenv())) {
    envs <- append(envs, environmentName(current))
    current <- parent.env(current)
  }
  paste(envs, collapse = " -> ")
}

# Environment snapshot
env_snapshot <- function(env) {
  list(
    objects = ls(envir = env),
    size = env_size(env),
    parent = environmentName(parent.env(env)),
    locked = environmentIsLocked(env)
  )
}
```

## Best Practices {#best-practices}

### 1. Consistent Naming

```r
# GOOD: Clear, consistent naming
.ConfigEnv <- new.env()
.CacheEnv <- new.env()
.StateEnv <- new.env()

# BAD: Inconsistent naming
config_env <- new.env()
CACHE <- new.env()
State.Environment <- new.env()
```

### 2. Appropriate Parent Selection

```r
# Configuration: isolated
.ConfigEnv <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())

# Module: needs global functions
.ModuleEnv <- new.env(parent = globalenv())

# Cache: isolated but could use global
.CacheEnv <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())
```

### 3. Cleanup and Lifecycle

```r
# Register cleanup
reg.finalizer(.CacheEnv, function(e) {
  # Cleanup code
  message("Cleaning up cache environment")
}, onexit = TRUE)

# Session cleanup
onSessionEnded(function() {
  env_clear(.CacheEnv)
  env_clear(.StateEnv)
})
```

### 4. Thread Safety Considerations

```r
# Use locks for concurrent access
.LockedEnv <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())
lockEnvironment(.LockedEnv, bindings = FALSE)

# Or use separate environments per session
create_session_env <- function(session_id) {
  env_name <- paste0(".Session", session_id, "Env")
  assign(env_name, new.env(parent = emptyenv()), 
         envir = .GlobalEnv)
  get(env_name)
}
```

## Common Pitfalls {#pitfalls}

### 1. Memory Leaks

```r
# BAD: Circular reference
.EnvA <- new.env()
.EnvB <- new.env()
.EnvA$ref <- .EnvB
.EnvB$ref <- .EnvA  # Circular reference!

# GOOD: Weak references or careful design
.EnvA <- new.env()
.EnvB <- new.env()
.EnvA$ref_id <- "EnvB"  # Store identifier instead
```

### 2. Global Namespace Pollution

```r
# BAD: Creating in global environment
my_env <- new.env()  # Pollutes global

# GOOD: Use consistent naming with period
.MyEnv <- new.env()  # Semi-private convention
```

### 3. Forgetting Parent Environment

```r
# BAD: Unexpected inheritance
.DataEnv <- new.env()  # Inherits from global!

# GOOD: Explicit parent
.DataEnv <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())  # No inheritance
```

## Testing Environments {#testing}

```r
test_that("environment isolation works", {
  .TestEnv <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())
  .TestEnv$x <- 10
  
  expect_equal(.TestEnv$x, 10)
  expect_false(exists("x"))  # Not in global
  expect_error(get("global_var", envir = .TestEnv))  # No access to global
})

test_that("environment inheritance works", {
  .ParentEnv <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())
  .ParentEnv$x <- 10
  
  .ChildEnv <- new.env(parent = .ParentEnv)
  expect_equal(get("x", envir = .ChildEnv), 10)
})
```

---

*This guide provides comprehensive patterns for using environments in R to achieve modularity, isolation, and clean code organization.*
